title: Substack vs Flodesk (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Flodesk email marketing tools. Learn pricing, features, and which platform suits your needs best.
intro: Substack and Flodesk serve different niches within email marketing. Substack excels at newsletter monetization with a built-in audience, while Flodesk focuses on visually stunning email design for creative professionals. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize direct subscriber revenue or beautiful, brand-aligned campaigns.
choose_a_if: You want to monetize newsletters directly from subscribers without upfront costs
You're a writer or content creator focused on building an audience community
You prefer a content-first platform with minimal design requirements
choose_b_if: You're a visual brand, designer, or photographer needing beautiful email templates
You want predictable flat-rate pricing without revenue-share arrangements
You need advanced features like workflows, checkout forms, and ecommerce integrations

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for writers monetizing content directly, while Flodesk is superior for visual brands and ecommerce. Choose based on your primary goal: subscriber revenue or beautiful campaign design.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Flodesk?
faq.a: Substack is free to start with no upfront costs, though you pay 10% of revenue from paid subscriptions. Flodesk requires a $38/month minimum investment. Substack is cheaper if you have no monetization, but Flodesk offers better value if you need advanced features without revenue concerns.

faq.q: Can I switch from Flodesk to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, most email lists can be exported and imported between platforms. However, Substack's focus on paid subscriptions and Flodesk's design-centric approach mean migrating may require restructuring your strategy rather than a simple technical transfer.
